prose:
Fukui reactivity indices for nitrobenzene were computed using DFT at the B3LYP/def2-TZVP level (tier standard) in the gas phase with charge 0 and multiplicity 1. The calculation employed PySCF with exact four-center integrals and no density fitting. The neutral, cation (N−1), and anion (N+1) states were all converged successfully. Results show the ortho-carbon to the nitro group (atom 4) as the most electrophilic site with f⁺ = 0.4964 and dual descriptor = 0.5107, while the meta-carbon (atom 5) is the most nucleophilic with f⁻ = 0.1073. The nitro group itself (N and both O atoms) exhibits electron-withdrawing character, with the oxygens showing positive dual descriptors (0.376–0.377) and the nitrogen a negative dual descriptor (−0.474). Fukui charge conservation checks passed: sum(f⁺) = 1.0000 and sum(f−) = 1.0000, confirming convergence of the N±1 states. The calculation is trustworthy per all integrity checks. The results are consistent with nitrobenzene's known ortho/para-deactivating behavior in electrophilic aromatic substitution.</prose>
<parameter name="integrity_trustworthy">true
